content: I think this is about deciding whether to do this at N or N-1

author: potuz
content: Oh crap FCU is really the problem! This is really a problem cause you can't decide your head until the next slot and you've counted attestations

author: potuz
content: I guess we're going to have to relax that?

author: potuz
content: I guess it's fine, just as today we will be forced to compute attributes and do the state transition for that, then send FCU and it head changes in the start of the slot send a new FCU

author: potuz
content: With no time

author: terencechain
content:
yes, this is what im dealing with now, there's two places typically, in prysm case
- we compute payload attribute and call FCU at n-1
- if there's a payload attribute cache miss (head changes last min), we compute payload attribute and call FCU at n right before proposing

author: nflaig
content: same in lodestar, I don't think this will change in gloas though

author: nflaig
content:
> we compute payload attribute and call FCU at n-1
timing of this will change, since payload might be at late as 9 seconds or so, I am not even sure yet what signal we should use to send the correct FCU, it seems to me that counting PTC votes is the right approach

author: potuz
content: PTC is a bad thing to do I believe

author: potuz
content: As of now, if you synced the Payload of your head it becomes your head

author: potuz
content: Regardless of PTC

author: potuz
content: There's some argument to be made about DA and not being a supernode, but could never made Francesco agree with it yet

author: terencechain
content:
depends on client implementation, whats interesting is you may not need to copy the state in `n-1` case because you wont need to save the post state anyway, the state is "thrown away"

and in `n` case i dont mind the copy because it's just a rare case to hit

author: tbenr
content:
Yes I was referring to the calculation of the withdrawals here:
```
apply_parent_execution_payload(state, parent_bid, envelope.execution_requests)
withdrawals = get_expected_withdrawals(state).withdrawals
```

author: nflaig
content:
so already pre-gloas the state we pass is already processed as noted in `bellatrix/validator.md`
```
`state` is the state object after applying `process_slots(state, slot)`
     transition to the resulting state of the parent block processing
```

`apply_parent_execution_payload` should be  pretty cheap as it doesn't change validators or balances

author: tbenr
content:
my default idea on that is that we will send FCU with attribute at payload arrival (or at PTC deadline) assuming we build on top of full, then at slot transition we recompute the head and eventually change our mind on the fly. 
And this could be a payload reorg or even a beacon block reorg.

This is also how it works today.

author: tbenr
content: (nowadays teku cheats a bit anticipating the process by 500ms)

author: potuz
content: what do you mean the copy is a rare case, the copy will be in the most common case

author: tbenr
content: right, much better indeed

author: potuz
content: Get head does not give you a state, just a root/node

author: nflaig
content: I really don't understand in current gloas spec where it is defined what state to pass into `prepare_execution_payload`, it could be a `block_state` or `payload_state`

author: potuz
content: The head state is the current beacon state at the tip of your canonical chain, and we always used that state advanced to the current slot to prepare a proposal. Now this is not enough

author: potuz
content: It is whatever your head view tells you, just as today, if your head is full it'll be the post EL state advanced. If it's empty it'll be the post CL state advanced

author: potuz
content: But now the state is not enough

author: potuz
content: Cause the state won't have enough info

author: tbenr
content: well, if you do a late block reorg you pick the state of the parent

author: nflaig
content: "tip of your canonical chain," how do you determine that in current gloas spec?

author: potuz
content: That's still compatible: your head is not the last block but an ancestor, now it's really becoming nitpicking semantics

author: potuz
content: The output of get_head tells you the block and the Payload content

author: nflaig
content: but that's exactly what I mean, `get_head` in gloas doesn't just mean you got the payload and advancted to a payload state, it runs `get_payload_status_tiebreaker` which requires PTC votes

author: potuz
content: Not, because the PB clause is always hit

author: potuz
content: So PTC is irrelevant

author: nflaig
content: ok so you're saying as soon as you have the payload imported you will consider that the tip of your canonical chain?

author: potuz
content: Yes if the Payload is the Payload of your head, not otherwise

author: potuz
content: It's the same as today with blocks. Any block that extends your head is your head

author: nflaig
content: following that reasoning doing `if hash_tree_root(state.latest_block_header) in store.payloads:` should be right

author: potuz
content: Barring honest reorgs

author: potuz
content: In that text I was pointing that you were enforcing that the Payload existed and nothing more, which works today if the state is your head state **only** because we haven't included clauses to reorg the Payload when PTC votes that DA is not available yet

author: potuz
content: That's why I want to make this full proof by asserting that the state must be head

author: potuz
content: Instead of just assuming it is if the Payload exists
